English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Bronfenbrenner Life Course Center records
New York State College of Human Ecology. Bronfenbrenner Life Course Center
Cornell Family Business Research Institute
Cornell Employment and Family Careers Institute
Cornell Gerontology Research Institute
Pathways to Life Quality Study
Cornell Institute for Translational Research on Aging
23-27-3234
2.4 cubic feet. 2.4 cubic feet.
1990-2004. Publications, office documents Includes historical information, committee meeting minutes, reports, publications and files concerning grants, symposiums and visiting scholars. Collection material in English 5179519
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Carrie Smith
Date completed:
October 2004
EAD encoding:
Peter Martinez, Nov. 2004
Date modified:
RMC Staff, March 2014
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
The Bronfenbrenner Life Course Center is a university-wide umbrella organization that houses the Cornell Institute for Translational Research on Aging. Past institutes included the Cornell Employment and Family Careers Institute, the Cornell Gerontology Research Institute and the Pathways to Life Quality Study. The center is named in honor of Urie Bronfenbrenner, Professor Emeritus of Human Development and Psychology at Cornell.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes historical information, committee meeting minutes, reports, publications and files concerning grants, symposiums and visiting scholars.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Grants
Series II. Charter
Series III. History
Series IV. Organizational Charts/Directories
Series V. Committees
Series VI. Reports/Plans
Series VII. Publicity
Series VIII. Publications
Series IX. Stationery
Series X. Distinguished Speakers and Visiting Scholars
Series XI. Colloquia/Symposia
Series XII. Family Business Research Institute/Cornell Employment and Family Careers Institute
General
Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.cornell.edu/handle/1813/11731
Cite As:
Bronfenbrenner Life Course Center records, #23-27-3234. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Families -- Study and teaching. Human ecology -- Research. Bronfenbrenner, Urie, 1917- New York State College of Human Ecology. Department of Human Development

Family Business Research Institute/ Cornell Employment and Family Careers Institute
Scope and Contents
During the mid to late 1990's, the Family Business Research Institute was a major institute within the Bronfenbrenner Life Course Center. FBRI was directed by Ramona Heck, J. Thomas Clark Professor of Entrepreneurship and Personal Enterprise. Ramona served on the Steering Committee for the Bronfenbrenner Life Course Center.Proposal - "A Life Course Approach to Working Families: A Research and Training Agenda" by Phyllis Moen1996325Family Business Research Institute Executive Summaries, Memos, Sample Focus Group Memo, Sample in Department Interview1990-1999326 ======= Series I.
